figures.caption: Figure 5: Comparison of our method with LLP-adapted SA-MIL and C2C on the in-house dataset. Red indicates high branching, green indicates low branching, and blue indicates sinusoid. Global class proportions are provided by an experienced pathologist and serve as the ground-truth labels. Predicted class proportions are computed by summing segmented pixels for each class. Notably, all of our models generate meaningful segmentation and accurate class proportions, enhancing trust in the predictions.
Table 4: Effect of patch size and stride on segmentation performance for the first-stage and second-stage VSLP variants.
Figure 4: Comparison of our method with the state-of-the-art approaches on samples from the BCSS [57] dataset.

Table 5: Effect of TTA rotation count on segmentation performance. The upper part reports performance as a function of the number of TTA rotations for different second-stage variants, using the best-performing first-stage patch size (224, 150). The lower part compares different first-stage backbone models under the same TTA setting.
Table 6: Performance of VSLP variants under different data conditions.
